pre_game_context.key_injury_shift: Jayson Tatum ruled out with left knee stiffness; Boston reshuffled lineup with Brown/White/Pritchard/Hauser/Queta taking larger usage and minutes.

market_context.pre_tatum_out_market: BOS spread showed as high as -7/-7.5 in sharp market screenshots; Kalshi lagged at BOS -5.5 earlier.
market_context.post_tatum_out_market: BOS repriced near -4.5/-165 with total around 205 after Tatum out confirmation.
market_context.sharpedge_interpretation: Tatum-out repricing created a manual LiveFlow pivot window toward PHI moneyline/spread/team-total rather than Boston favorite exposure.

investment_summary.largest_positive_driver: PHI moneyline +134 and PHI team total over 105.5
investment_summary.largest_negative_driver: Paul George role props; production concentrated through Embiid, Maxey, Edgecombe instead.

pre_tatum_out_state.core_assumption: Boston spacing/3PT volume remains viable if Tatum active or limited-active.

post_tatum_out_reassessment.spread_direction: PHI + points, then PHI alt margin ladder after game-flow confirmation
post_tatum_out_reassessment.team_total_direction: PHI TT Over strengthened as Embiid/Maxey became control engine
post_tatum_out_reassessment.full_total_direction: Total stayed less attractive than PHI-specific exposure because Boston efficiency risk remained high.

final_model_scorecard.mean_projection: PHI by 1.5 to 3.5 before live separation

final_model_scorecard.model_probability_estimate: 60-64% at halftime/live entry after PHI led 55-50
Thin plus-money ladder prior to role compression
Thin plus-money ladder prior to final role confirmation

q1.signal: Philadelphia seized immediate control with Embiid/Maxey creation and Boston's Tatum-less spacing exposed early.
q1.model_note: Early 15-point PHI lead confirmed injury-reprice edge and pushed Boston into high-variance 3PT chase mode.

q2.signal: Boston bench/guard shot-making stabilized the game; White and Queta created a temporary counterpunch.
q2.model_note: This quarter prevented an easy PHI runaway and kept live team total/spread entries price-sensitive.

q3.signal: Philadelphia restored separation with 57.1% FG and 66.7% 3PT shooting in the quarter while Boston shot 33.3% FG and 23.5% from three.
q3.model_note: This was the decisive model-validation window for PHI TT and PHI spread ladder.

q4.signal: Boston cut the lead to one multiple times, but Maxey's late drives/free throws closed the game.
q4.model_note: PHI TT Over was saved and validated through late foul-extension and Maxey's 6-for-6 fourth-quarter FT profile.

pivotal_moments.impact: Converted matchup from Boston favorite profile into PHI plus-money upset and team-total hunting window.
Confirmed Boston's half-court creation stress without Tatum.

pivotal_moments.event: VJ Edgecombe three pushes PHI lead to 18
pivotal_moments.impact: Stretched Boston into late chase mode and validated PHI margin control.

pivotal_moments.event: Boston cuts deficit to 1 multiple times
pivotal_moments.impact: Created sweat-zone for spread/ML but did not break PHI due to Embiid and Maxey stabilization.

pivotal_moments.event: Maxey and Edgecombe close at the line
pivotal_moments.impact: PHI TT Over 105.5 and PHI -4.5 both cash.

wins: PHI ML hit because Tatum's absence removed Boston's primary pressure valve and compressed offense into Brown/White high-volume creation.
PHI -4.5 hit because Philadelphia generated three separate control windows: opening burst, third-quarter separation, and late free throw close.
PHI TT Over 105.5 hit because Maxey/Embiid created enough late foul pressure despite a cold fourth-quarter field-goal rate.
misses: Paul George 20+ missed because he was not the primary usage beneficiary; Embiid, Maxey, and Edgecombe absorbed the scoring profile.
Paul George 4+ assists missed because playmaking concentrated through Maxey and Embiid; George finished with only one assist.
hit_miss_reason.model_assessment: The macro read was excellent after Tatum was ruled out; the weak point was overextending into Paul George role props instead of prioritizing PHI team-level derivatives and Maxey/Embiid control props.

model_patch_recommendations.description: When an opposing star is ruled out, reassign edge priority first to team-level markets, then to the highest-touch ball-handlers/bigs, and downgrade tertiary player props unless direct on/off usage evidence supports them.
For plus-money player ladders, require confirmed touch/assist role evidence after final lineups when the player is not the primary ball-handler or post hub.
In Game 7 elimination settings, boost team-total overs when live spread remains within 6-12 points and opponent is forced into late take-foul mode.
When Tatum is inactive, raise variance on Boston team totals and full-game overs if 3PA share exceeds 45% of FGA and Brown/White combined shot burden exceeds 45 FGA.

PHI: Embiid remains primary playoff control hub; model should treat him as points/rebounds/assists stabilizer but monitor late-game fatigue and efficiency dips.
Maxey is the closing engine; late-game FT and drive volume deserve elevated live-market priority.
Edgecombe spacing spike is real enough to log as a volatility upside tag, especially when PHI needs secondary shot creation.
Paul George remains valuable as spacing/defensive glue, but not automatically as a usage or assist ladder target.
BOS: Tatum absence materially changes offensive geometry; Boston becomes Brown/White/Pritchard high-3PA profile with efficiency volatility.
Queta rebounding/minute role was validated off bench despite not starting: 32:33, 17 points, 12 rebounds.
Boston can still generate comeback runs, but without Tatum their late-game shot quality narrows.
